devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वृत्रे हते त्रयो लोका विना शक्रेण भूरिद । सपाला ह्यभवन् सद्यो विज्वरा निर्वृतेन्द्रियाः ॥ 1 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vṛtre hate trayo lokā vinā śakreṇa bhūrida sapālā hyabhavan sadyo vijvarā nirvṛtendriyāḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When Vṛtra was killed, all the three worlds along with their guardian deities, with the exception of Indra, became immediately free from feverish anxiety and felt felicitous at heart.

devanagari: देवर्षिपितृभूतानि दैत्या देवानुगाः स्वयम् । प्रतिजग्मुः स्वधिष्ण्यानि ब्रह्मेशेन्द्रादयस्ततः ॥ 2 ॥
english_devnagari: devarṣipitṛbhūtāni daityā devānugāḥ svayam pratijagmuḥ svadhiṣṇyāni brahmeśendrādayastataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Without bidding good-bye to Indra, gods, sages, manes, Bhūtas, demons and the followers of gods returned to their respective regions. Then dispersed Brahmā, Rudra, Indra and others.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Anvitārthaprakāśikā, Indra did not return to Svarga. The very moment he killed Vṛtra, the sin of killing a Brāhmaṇa (Brahma-hatyā) assailed him and Indra had to fly away hurriedly and hide himself in the Mānasa lake. Indra’s restoration took place after his performance of a horse sacrifice.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । इन्द्रस्यानिर्वृतेर्हेतुं श्रोतुमिच्छामि भो मुने । येनासन् सुखिनो देवा हरेर्दुःखं कुतोऽभवत् ॥ 3 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca indrasyānirvṛterhetuṃ śrotumicchāmi bho mune yenāsan sukhino devā harerduḥkhaṃ kuto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King said:
Oh Sage, I wish to hear the cause of Indra’s unhappiness whereby gods became happy, while Indra suffered agony.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वृत्रविक्रमसंविग्नाः सर्वे देवाः सहर्षिभिः । तद्वधायार्थयन्निन्द्रं नैच्छद्भीतो बृहद्वधात् ॥ 4 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vṛtravikramasaṃvignāḥ sarve devāḥ saharṣibhiḥ tadvadhāyārthayannindraṃ naicchadbhīto bṛhadvadhāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Allgods along with sages who were agitated over the prowess of Vṛtra, begged Indra to slay him, but Indra being afraid of Brahma-hatyā did not wish to do so.

devanagari: इन्द्र उवाच । स्त्रीभूद्रुमजलैरेनो विश्वरूपवधोद्भवम् । विभक्तमनुगृह्णद्भिर्वृत्रहत्यां क्व मार्ज्म्यहम् ॥ 5 ॥
english_devnagari: indra uvāca strībhūdrumajalaireno viśvarūpavadhodbhavam vibhaktamanugṛhṇadbhirvṛtrahatyāṃ kva mārjmyaham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Indra said:
The sin of killing Viśvarūpa, a Brāhmaṇa, was graciously shared by women, the earth, the water and trees. Where can I wipe off the sin of killing Vṛtra, another Brāhmaṇa?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । ऋषयस्तदुपाकर्ण्य महेन्द्रमिदमब्रुवन् । याजयिष्याम भद्रं ते हयमेधेन मा स्म भैः ॥ 6 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ṛṣayastadupākarṇya mahendramidamabruvan yājayiṣyāma bhadraṃ te hayamedhena mā sma bhaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having listened to him, the sages assured him, “May good betide you! We shall help you in performing a horse- sacrifice. Please do not get afraid (of Brahma-hatyā).

devanagari: हयमेधेन पुरुषं परमात्मानमीश्वरम् । इष्ट्वा नारायणं देवं मोक्ष्यसेऽपि जगद्वधात् ॥ 7 ॥
english_devnagari: hayamedhena puruṣaṃ paramātmānamīśvaram iṣṭvā nārāyaṇaṃ devaṃ mokṣyase
translation: Worshipping Lord Nārāyaṇa, the Supreme Man, the universal Soul and the Controller of the Universe, by performance of the Horse-sacrifice, you will be absolved of the sin of massacring the world. What of one Brāhmaṇa like Vṛtra.

devanagari: ब्रह्महा पितृहा गोघ्नो मातृहाचार्यहाघवान् । श्वादः पुल्कसको वापि शुद्ध्येरन् यस्य कीर्तनात् ॥ 8 ॥
english_devnagari: brahmahā pitṛhā goghno mātṛhācāryahāghavān śvādaḥ pulkasako vāpi śuddhyeran yasya kīrtanāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ir class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ ir class 6 verb]

translation: By singing the Lord’s name, a person who slays a Brāhmaṇa or his own father, or a cow, or his own mother, or a preceptor, a sinner who eats a dog, or is of the lowest caste, becomes purified.

devanagari: तमश्वमेधेन महामखेन श्रद्धान्वितोऽस्माभिरनुष्ठितेन । हत्वापि सब्रह्मचराचरं त्वं न लिप्यसे किं खलनिग्रहेण ॥ 9 ॥
english_devnagari: tamaśvamedhena mahāmakhena śraddhānvito hatvāpi sabrahmacarācaraṃ tvaṃ na lipyase kiṃ khalanigraheṇa
translation: If with faith and reverence, the great sacrifice called Aśvamedha (Horse-sacrifice) be performed with our co-operation, you will not be affected by sin, even if you kill all the mobile and immobile creation along with Brahmā. Then what of killing a wicked demon like Vṛtra?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं सञ्चोदितो विप्रैर्मरुत्वानहनद्रिपुम् । ब्रह्महत्या हते तस्मिन्नाससाद वृषाकपिम् ॥ 10 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ sañcodito viprairmarutvānahanadripum brahmahatyā hate tasminnāsasāda vṛṣākapim

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus strongly impelled by Brāhmaṇas, Indra killed his enemy Vṛtra. As soon as Vṛtra was killed, the sin of slaying a Brāhmaṇa attacked Indra.

devanagari: तयेन्द्रः स्मासहत्तापं निर्वृतिर्नामुमाविशत् । ह्रीमन्तं वाच्यतां प्राप्तं सुखयन्त्यपि नो गुणाः ॥ 11 ॥
english_devnagari: tayendraḥ smāsahattāpaṃ nirvṛtirnāmumāviśat hrīmantaṃ vācyatāṃ prāptaṃ sukhayantyapi no guṇāḥ

translation: Indra suffered agonies by that and did not get any peace of mind, for to selfrespecting persons, excellences like sovereignty do not give any pleasure if they bring censure.

devanagari: तां ददर्शानुधावन्तीं चाण्डालीमिव रूपिणीम् । जरया वेपमानाङ्गीं यक्ष्मग्रस्तामसृक्पटाम् ॥ 12 ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ dadarśānudhāvantīṃ cāṇḍālīmiva rūpiṇīm jarayā vepamānāṅgīṃ yakṣmagrastāmasṛkpaṭām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He saw the Brahmahatyā chasing after him. She was like a Cāṇḍāla woman in form. Her body was trembling with age and she was a victim of consumption. She put on blood-stained garments. With her grey hair scattered, she was asking Indra to stop and stand. She was polluting the atmosphere on the road with her breath stinking like rotten fish.

devanagari: विकीर्य पलितान् केशांस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति भाषिणीम् । मीनगन्ध्यसुगन्धेन कुर्वतीं मार्गदूषणम् ॥ 13 ॥
english_devnagari: vikīrya palitān keśāṃstiṣṭha tiṣṭheti bhāṣiṇīm mīnagandhyasugandhena kurvatīṃ mārgadūṣaṇam

translation: He saw the Brahmahatyā chasing after him. She was like a Cāṇḍāla woman in form. Her body was trembling with age and she was a victim of consumption. She put on blood-stained garments. With her grey hair scattered, she was asking Indra to stop and stand. She was polluting the atmosphere on the road with her breath stinking like rotten fish.

devanagari: नभो गतो दिशः सर्वाः सहस्राक्षो विशाम्पते । प्रागुदीचीं दिशं तूर्णं प्रविष्टो नृप मानसम् ॥ 14 ॥
english_devnagari: nabho gato diśaḥ sarvāḥ sahasrākṣo viśāmpate prāgudīcīṃ diśaṃ tūrṇaṃ praviṣṭo nṛpa mānasam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: The thousand-eyed god ran in all directions in the sky, Oh Lord of the earth. He speedily rushed to the northeast and entered the Mānasa lake, Oh king.

devanagari: स आवसत्पुष्करनालतन्तूनलब्धभोगो यदिहाग्निदूतः । वर्षाणि साहस्रमलक्षितोऽन्तः सञ्चिन्तयन् ब्रह्मवधाद्विमोक्षम् ॥ 15 ॥
english_devnagari: sa āvasatpuṣkaranālatantūnalabdhabhogo yadihāgnidūtaḥ varṣāṇi sāhasramalakṣito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He stayed within the fibres of a lotus therein without getting any subsistence, for he had the fire-god as his purveyor of food.[2] He remained unperceived in the water for one thousand years, pondering over his absolution from the sin of Brahma-hatyā.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā points out the impossibility of fire-god’s entry into water. VC. & Anvitārthaprakāśikā. attribute his non-entry to the guards of Rudra, for Agni purveys oblations of Varuṇa.

devanagari: तावत्त्रिणाकं नहुषः शशास विद्यातपोयोगबलानुभावः । स सम्पदैश्वर्यमदान्धबुद्धिर्नीतस्तिरश्चां गतिमिन्द्रपत्न्या ॥ 16 ॥
english_devnagari: tāvattriṇākaṃ nahuṣaḥ śaśāsa vidyātapoyogabalānubhāvaḥ sa sampadaiśvaryamadāndhabuddhirnītastiraścāṃ gatimindrapatnyā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: During that period (of Indra’s absence), Nahuṣa who possessed the capacity of a ruler due to his knowledge, austerities and strength of yoga, governed the third heaven. But as his intelligence became blind by the arrogance of affluence, and power was reduced to the state of sub-human being (boa constrictor) by Indra’s wife, Śacī.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Nahuṣa thought that as be was Indra, former Indra’s wife would be ipso facto his own. When Śacī received an invitation from Nahuṣa, she, as per advice of Bṛhaspati. requested him to come to her in a palanquine borne by sages like Agastya. Nahuṣa while being borne thus, in his eagerness, touched Agastya with his leg and asked him to hasten (Sarpa, Sarpa). Agastya being thus insulted cursed him to be a serpent.

devanagari: ततो गतो ब्रह्मगिरोपहूत ऋतम्भरध्याननिवारिताघः । पापस्तु दिग्देवतया हतौजास्तं नाभ्यभूदवितं विष्णुपत्न्या ॥ 17 ॥
english_devnagari: tato gato brahmagiropahūta ṛtambharadhyānanivāritāghaḥ pāpastu digdevatayā hataujāstaṃ nābhyabhūdavitaṃ viṣṇupatnyā
translation: Then Indra whose sin was warded off by meditation over the protector of Truth, namely, Lord Viṣṇu, returned as he was invited by Brāhmaṇas. His sin of Brahma-hatyā was deprived of its power by Rudra, the god of that direction, and it did not assail him as he was protected by Lakṣmī, spouse of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: तं च ब्रह्मर्षयोऽभ्येत्य हयमेधेन भारत । यथावद्दीक्षयां चक्रुः पुरुषाराधनेन ह ॥ 18 ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ ca brahmarṣayo yathāvaddīkṣayāṃ cakruḥ puruṣārādhanena ha
translation: The Brāhmaṇa sages approached him and consecrated him according to religious formalities for the performance of the Horse-Sacrifice by which Hari is propitiated.

devanagari: अथेज्यमाने पुरुषे सर्वदेवमयात्मनि । अश्वमेधे महेन्द्रेण वितते ब्रह्मवादिभिः ॥ 19 ॥
english_devnagari: athejyamāne puruṣe sarvadevamayātmani aśvamedhe mahendreṇa vitate brahmavādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: When the Supreme Person who embodies within himself all the divinities, was thus worshipped by the great Indra, in the Horse-Sacrifice which was performed duly through the Brāhmaṇas who were expositors of the Veda, the huge mass of sin of slaying Vṛtra the son of Tvaṣṭṛ was reduced to naught by that very worship of the Lord just as the frost is evaporated by the Sun.

devanagari: स वै त्वाष्ट्रवधो भूयानपि पापचयो नृप । नीतस्तेनैव शून्याय नीहार इव भानुना ॥ 20 ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai tvāṣṭravadho bhūyānapi pāpacayo nṛpa nītastenaiva śūnyāya nīhāra iva bhānunā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When the Supreme Person who embodies within himself all the divinities, was thus worshipped by the great Indra, in the Horse-Sacrifice which was performed duly through the Brāhmaṇas who were expositors of the Veda, the huge mass of sin of slaying Vṛtra the son of Tvaṣṭṛ was reduced to naught by that very worship of the Lord just as the frost is evaporated by the Sun.

devanagari: स वाजिमेधेन यथोदितेन वितायमानेन मरीचिमिश्रैः । इष्ट्वाधियज्ञं पुरुषं पुराणमिन्द्रो महानास विधूतपापः ॥ 21 ॥
english_devnagari: sa vājimedhena yathoditena vitāyamānena marīcimiśraiḥ iṣṭvādhiyajñaṃ puruṣaṃ purāṇamindro mahānāsa vidhūtapāpaḥ
translation: By performance of the Horse-sacrifice according to scriptural injunctions through sages like Marīci who conducted it on proper lines, Indra worshipped the ancient Supreme Person, the presiding deity of sacrifices and thus being completely free from sin (of Brahmahatyā), became as great as before.

devanagari: इदं महाख्यानमशेषपाप्मनां प्रक्षालनं तीर्थपदानुकीर्तनम् । भक्त्युच्छ्रयं भक्तजनानुवर्णनं महेन्द्रमोक्षं विजयं मरुत्वतः ॥ 22 ॥
english_devnagari: idaṃ mahākhyānamaśeṣapāpmanāṃ prakṣālanaṃ tīrthapadānukīrtanam bhaktyucchrayaṃ bhaktajanānuvarṇanaṃ mahendramokṣaṃ vijayaṃ marutvataḥ
translation: This great narrative which celebrates the glory of Lord Viṣṇu of hallowed feet, absolves one of all sins. It extols the virtues of devotion and the greatness of the devotees of the Lord, and describes the purification of the great Indra from Biahma-hatyā and his final victory.

devanagari: पठेयुराख्यानमिदं सदा बुधाः शृण्वन्त्यथो पर्वणि पर्वणीन्द्रियम् । धन्यं यशस्यं निखिलाघमोचनं रिपुञ्जयं स्वस्त्ययनं तथायुषम् ॥ 23 ॥
english_devnagari: paṭheyurākhyānamidaṃ sadā budhāḥ śṛṇvantyatho parvaṇi parvaṇīndriyam dhanyaṃ yaśasyaṃ nikhilāghamocanaṃ ripuñjayaṃ svastyayanaṃ tathāyuṣam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation:
Wise persons should always read this narrative. They should listen to it on the days of Parvans e.g. the new- moon-day and the full-moon-day. It contributes to the prosperity and glory, removes sins, bestows victories over enemies, is conducive to auspiciousness and confers long life.
